message:
feat(python): numpy.tensordot wrapper parity (franken_numpy-aa63)

Add fnp_python.tensordot(a, b, axes=2) as a passthrough delegator
to np.tensordot. The `axes` argument accepts both forms:
  - int N (sum over last N axes of a vs first N of b)
  - 2-tuple of axis sequences (explicit per-side contraction)
Default axes=2 matches numpy's signature exactly.

Covered by one pyo3 oracle test with five scenarios: default
axes=2 on a (2,3,4)·(3,4,5) tensor pair, axes=1 (matmul-equivalent)
on (2,3)·(3,4), axes=0 (full outer product) on two 1-D vectors,
2-tuple of axis sequences ([1],[0]) on the same matrix pair, and
a complex128 axes=1 contraction. Each compared via numpy.allclose
against np.tensordot.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
